title: brachylaena neriifolia
intro: Brachylaena neriifolia, also known as the bush ironwood or ironwood tree, is a plant species that belongs to the Asteraceae family, native to southern Africa. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Brachylaena neriifolia include treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as reducing fever and relieving pain. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and bark are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
